project.title: GST Pull-down-MS identify candidate host factors that interact with CHIKV nsP4
project.projectDescription: The Chikungunya virus (CHIKV) is a single positive-stranded RNA virus transmitted by mosquitoes. More than 40 countries have reported cases of CHIKV infection to varying degrees. Currently, there is no licensed specific antiviral drug or vaccine for CHIKV in the world. In this study, we have identified host protein interactors of CHIKV nsP4 proteins in 293T cells and the major cellular pathways involved in the interaction between host factors and CHIKV nsp4 using Co-Immunoprecipitation and GST Pull-down followed by high-throughput quantitative mass spectrometry.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: GST pull-down experiment was conducted via the BeyoGold™ GST-tag Purification Resin (BeyoGold) according to the recommended protocol. Briefly, 50% BeyoGold™ GST-tag Purification Resin was completely resuspended by gently shaking and an appropriate amount of 50% gel suspension was transferred into a centrifuge tube (50μl 50% gel suspension for each sample). GST pull-down protein binding buffer  (20 times the volume of 50% gel suspension, e.g. 1ml protein binding buffer for 50μl gel suspension) was used to wash and balance the resin by mixing upside down and centrifugation at 1000g for 2min. After removing the supernatant, the remaining resin was resuspended with the equal volume of GST pull-down protein binding buffer to the resin (for example, 50μl 50% gel suspension is re-suspended with 25μl GST pull-down protein binding buffer), which is ready for GST pull-down experiment. For each reaction, 400 ul GST Pull-down protein binding buffer, 50ul balanced 50% resin suspension, 25ug cell lysate, and 25ug GST-tagged RdRp protein (decoy protein) or GST protein (negative control) were added to a Ep tube in sequence, supplemented with GST Pull-down protein binding buffer to make the same volume of each sample (0.5-1ml). After mixing upside down, the tube was placed on a side shaker and incubated at 4ºC for 2 hours or overnight. The mixture was centrifuged at 1000g at 4ºC for 2min. 100μl GST Pull-down protein binding buffer was used to resuspend the resin that was centrifuged at 4ºC (1000g×10s) to fully wash off the unbound protein, which was repeated twice. Finally, 50μl GST pull-down buffer was added to each reaction, which was gently shaked and incubated for 10min, followed by centrifugation at 1000g at 4ºC for 2min. The proteins pulled down by GST-RdRp fusion protein in the supernatant was harvested for subsequent analysis.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Mass spectrometry analysis was conducted by the company APTBIO (Shanghai, China). Briefly, samples were firstly treated with reagents for reduction and alkylation, followed by enzymatic hydrolysis at 37℃ for 20 hours. The hydrolyzed samples were freeze-dried after desalting, dissolved in 0.1%FA solution, and stored at -20℃  for use. Samples were loaded to the chromatographic Trap column balanced with 95% liquid A (0.1% formic acid aqueous solution) for mass spectrometry analysis. The raw file of mass spectrometry test (raw file) uses the search engine Proteome Discoverer 2.5 to search the corresponding database, and finally obtains the identified protein results. Search database parameters: enzyme (Trypsin), database (uniprot_Homo_ sapiens_ 203800_ 20220104), fixed modification (Carbamidomethy1 (C)), variable modification (Carbamidomethy1 (M)), the maximum missing site was 2, and the confidence of the screening peptide was high.
